name: Cycle Diagram — Scientific Method Iterative Process
input: Show the iterative cycle of the scientific method: Observation leads to Hypothesis, which leads to Experiment, then Data Analysis, then Conclusion, and back to Observation for the next cycle.
qualityCriteria: Items present (Observation, Hypothesis, Experiment, Analysis, Conclusion)
Description for each item is present
No placeholder or lorem ipsum content
napkinBenchmark: Circular diagram with 5 nodes arranged in a ring, arrows connecting each to the next, final arrow loops back to first. Each node has an icon and short description.

data.title: The Scientific Method Cycle

items.label: Observation
items.description: Identify a phenomenon or pattern in the natural world that requires explanation

items.label: Hypothesis
items.description: Formulate a testable prediction based on observations and existing knowledge

items.label: Experiment
items.description: Design and conduct controlled experiments to test the hypothesis

items.label: Data Analysis
items.description: Collect, organize, and statistically analyze experimental results

items.label: Conclusion
items.description: Interpret results, accept or reject hypothesis, and identify new questions

data.caption: The iterative cycle of scientific inquiry
